I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix: **Fix 1** (Line 224): `Button variant="plain"` — The type changed to add `'stateful'` but `'plain'` is still valid. No code change needed. **Fix 2** (Line 224): Button with `{label}` as children. `{label}` is a text label, not an icon element. The rule says to move icon content to the `icon` prop. Since this Button contains only text (`{label}`), not an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons`, no change is needed. **Fix 3** (Lines 280, 290, 300, 308, 330, 340): `DescriptionListTerm` with icon content. Looking at each: - Line 280: ` {t('Critical: ')}` — icon AND text, move icon to `icon` prop - Line 290: ` {t('Warning: ')}` — icon AND text, move icon to `icon` prop - Line 300: ` {t('Info: ')}` — icon AND text, move icon to `icon` prop - Line 308: ` {t('None: ')}` — icon AND text, move icon to `icon` prop - Line 330: `{t('Platform: ')}` — no icon, no change needed - Line 340: `{t('User: ')}` — no icon, no change needed **Fix 4** (Line 21): Popover behavioral change regarding Popper's `appendTo` default. This is an informational/test-related warning about the Popover component. No code change needed to the source file — this would only affect tests. **Fix 5** (Line 214): `` needs a `label` prop. The `Severity` component defined in this very file (line 112) is a local component, not from `@openshift-console/dynamic-plugin-sdk`. Looking at the import list, `Severity` is NOT imported — it's defined locally. The rule's incident context says `parentFrom: @openshift-console/dynamic-plugin-sdk`, but the component being used is the local `Severity` component. Since this is a locally-defined component, the external SDK rule doesn't apply. However, let me check if there's a different `Severity` from the SDK... No, the `Severity` used at line 214 is the locally defined one at line 112. No change needed. **Fix 6** (Line 214): Same situation — `severity` prop is already being passed (`severity={severity}`). Already applied/not needed. Now let me apply the changes. The only real changes are for Fix 3, lines 280, 290, 300, 308 (moving SeverityIcon to the `icon` prop).

content.text:
Everything looks correct. The file is in good shape.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (line 224)
**No change needed.** The value `"plain"` is still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change was the addition of `'stateful'`, which doesn't affect existing usage.

### Fix 2 — Icon children to `icon` prop on Button (line 224)
**No change needed.** The Button at line 224 contains `{label}` (a text string), not an icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The rule only applies when the Button contains icon elements.

### Fix 3 — Icon children to `icon` prop on DescriptionListTerm (lines 280, 290, 300, 308, 330, 340)
**Applied (4 of 6 locations).** Moved `<SeverityIcon>` from children to the `icon` prop on DescriptionListTerm at lines 280, 290, 300, and 308. Lines 330 and 340 had no icon content (only `<strong>` text), so no change was needed there.

### Fix 4 — Popover transitive behavioral change (line 21)
**No change needed.** This is an informational warning about Popover's internal Popper sub-component changing its `appendTo` default. No source code change is required — this only affects test files that assert on portal/DOM placement behavior.

### Fix 5 — Required prop `label` on Severity (line 214)
**No change needed.** The `<Severity>` component at line 214 is the locally-defined component (line 112 of this same file), not an external SDK component. The local component's props interface (`{ severity: string }`) does not require a `label` prop.

### Fix 6 — Required prop `severity` on Severity (line 214)
**No change needed.** The `severity` prop is already being passed: `<Severity severity={severity} />`.

Everything looks correct. The file is in good shape.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant` type changed (line 224)
**No change needed.** The value `"plain"` is still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change was the addition of `'stateful'`, which doesn't affect existing usage.

### Fix 2 — Icon children to `icon` prop on Button (line 224)
**No change needed.** The Button at line 224 contains `{label}` (a text string), not an icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The rule only applies when the Button contains icon elements.

### Fix 3 — Icon children to `icon` prop on DescriptionListTerm (lines 280, 290, 300, 308, 330, 340)
**Applied (4 of 6 locations).** Moved `<SeverityIcon>` from children to the `icon` prop on DescriptionListTerm at lines 280, 290, 300, and 308. Lines 330 and 340 had no icon content (only `<strong>` text), so no change was needed there.

### Fix 4 — Popover transitive behavioral change (line 21)
**No change needed.** This is an informational warning about Popover's internal Popper sub-component changing its `appendTo` default. No source code change is required — this only affects test files that assert on portal/DOM placement behavior.

### Fix 5 — Required prop `label` on Severity (line 214)
**No change needed.** The `<Severity>` component at line 214 is the locally-defined component (line 112 of this same file), not an external SDK component. The local component's props interface (`{ severity: string }`) does not require a `label` prop.

### Fix 6 — Required prop `severity` on Severity (line 214)
**No change needed.** The `severity` prop is already being passed: `<Severity severity={severity} />`.
